Description: Add 0 to a register?
Adds together the values contained in  Register 1 and Register 2, and put the result in the outR
Subtracts the value contained within Register 1 from the value contained within Register 2 and places the result within the out Register.
Multiplies the value contained within Register 1 from the value contained within Register 2 and places the result within the out Register.
Divides the value contained within Register 1 from the value contained within Register 2 and places the result within the out Register.
Checks if the value of R1 and R2 are equal and outputs the value as 0 or 1 to the out Register.
Checks if the value of R1 is greater than the value of R2 and outputs true/false (1, 0) to the outR.
Checks if the value of R1 is less than than the value of R2 and outputs true/false (1, 0) to the outR.
Sets the val'th bit (starting at least significant bit) to 1 in the out register.
Sets the val'th bit (starting at least significant bit) to 0 in the out register.
Sets the value contained within a given  Register, R1 to the value provided.
Moves the value contained within a given Register, 2R, and copies/moves it to the given register, outR
Checks if the print bit(first bit) of flag register is set to 1, sets it to 0, and writes the char register to console.
If a key is pressed, it sets the value within the flag register to 1 and the val register to the current key.
If the rndm flag is 1, set to 0, set rndm register to val inbetween minv and maxv
Pushes the value contained within R1 onto the stack: writes R1 to the memory address held in the Stack Pointer, then moves the Stack Pointer down by 1.
Pops the top value off the stack into R1: moves the Stack Pointer up by 1, then reads the value at the Stack Pointer address into R1.
Loads into R0 the value stored in RAM at address RamAddr.
Stores the value in R0 into RAM at address RamAddr.
Increment the value contained within the given register, R1 by 1.
Decrement the value contained within the given register, R1, by 1.
Loads into R0 the value stored in RAM at the address held in R1 (indirect load).
Stores the value in R0 into RAM at the address held in R1 (indirect store).
Sets R1 to the value contained in R0 (register-to-register copy).
Nots the value within R1 and outputs the value in outR
Ands the values contained within R1 and R2 and outputs their result to outR.
Ors the values contained within R1 and R2 and outputs their result to outR.
Nors the values contained within R1 and R2 and outputs their result to outR.
Nands the values contained within R1 and R2 and outputs their result to outR.
Xors the values contained within R1 and R2 and outputs their result to outR.
Left shifts the value in R1 by value contained within R2 and outputs in outR.
Change the instruction pointer to the first instruction after a given label address.
Change the instruction pointer to the first instruction after a given label address IF the value in R1 equals 0.
Change the instruction pointer to the first instruction after a given label address if the two register values are equal.
Sets the instruction pointer to the value stored in R0 (indirect jump).
